working_on: Pre-commit STATUS-capture bug + side-discovery audit + scheduling-barrier fix per HANDOFF.md S-0033 entry.
outcome_summary: Three coupled mechanical fixes per HANDOFF.md S-0033 entry. Item 1: pre-commit hook STATUS=$? capture bug (cmd || STATUS=$? pattern); smoke-test verified blocks on a deliberate mypy --strict violation. Item 2: side-discovery audit at session shutdown — new engine/tools/audit_side_discoveries.py + 22-test suite, new step 6 in shutdown sequence (renumbered 6→7, 7→8, 8→9, 9→10), Skill mirror updated, ENGINE_LOG entries. Item 3: engine/scheduled_audits.json added to exploration-mode allowed-paths. Cold-review pass (Python/engine): 2 file(s), all match contract. Side-discovery audit dogfood passed at close: 1 marker dispositioned (acceptable_no_action — self-referential phrase 'follow-up markers' in Item 2 commit body describing the audit vocabulary itself). Phase 4 build-readiness gate exercise defers from S-0033 to S-0034. In-session anomaly: parent's core.bare=true blocked worktree git operations partway through; fixed inline via core.bare=false in this worktree's config.worktree; captured in HANDOFF.md as a follow-up. Validator clean: 14 checks, 0 hard-fails, 0 soft-warns. Pytest 154 passing.

side_discoveries.reasoning: Self-referential false positive — the Item 2 commit body describes the new audit's marker vocabulary (the literal phrase 'follow-up markers in commit messages' appears in body text introducing the audit purpose). Not flagging an actual follow-up; flagging the existence of follow-up-marker scanning. The audit catches it correctly as a marker; disposition records that the appearance is documentation about the mechanism, not an unsurfaced action.
